title: Dementia awareness resources toolkit: Welcome - Canada.ca
content:
## Dementia awareness resources toolkit: Welcome
- Dementia-inclusive communities
Download the alternative format (PDF format, 3.67 MB, 39 pages)
Organization: Public Health Agency of Canada

## Welcome to the dementia awareness resources toolkit
This digital toolkit was created to share dementia information and resources developed during the Public Health Agency of Canada's national public education campaign (2019–2024). Resources were developed to expand awareness of dementia risk reduction, reduce stigma and enable communities to be more dementia-inclusive.
This content can be shared with your network through social media and/or other channels, and it can be used to inspire your own content to raise awareness of dementia. Resources are available in English and French.

## Exploring the toolkit
The toolkit offers awareness resources and information on each topic below such as videos, images and social media content.
Find links to dementia information, resources and key documents, including Canada's national dementia strategy and annual reports to Parliament.
Discover information and resources related to reducing the risk of developing dementia.
Explore how to reduce stigma to help improve the quality of life of people living with dementia, their family and caregivers.
Dementia-inclusive communities
Learn more about creating dementia-inclusive communities, which can support independence and engagement for people living with dementia.

## How to use the resources

### Sharing images and videos:
- Download the files or copy the links to use them in your own communication activities.

### Creating social media posts:
- You can download the image and  copy and paste the accompanying text to create a new post on your social media  channels or other communication activities.
The sharing of social media posts directly from the Public Health Agency of Canada's official accounts is encouraged (X [formerly Twitter]: @GovCanHealth; Facebook: @Healthy Canadians; LinkedIn: @Public Health Agency of Canada/Agence de la santé publique du Canada). These posts may be reproduced, in whole or in part, without permission when used for non-commercial purposes only, provided the source is fully acknowledged (Source: Public Health Agency of Canada, Canada.ca/dementia).
For commercial requests or if you want to make significant changes to our images or text to create new content, please submit your request for copyright permission, using the following form: Application for Copyright Clearance on Health Canada Works
